name: Typeclasses / Traits / Interfaces
def: A mechanism for ad-hoc polymorphism: defining behaviour a type must provide, dispatched statically or dynamically.
problem: Write code against capabilities rather than concrete types, and add behaviour to existing types.
mechanism: A trait/interface declares methods; types implement it. Dispatch is static (monomorphised, generics) or dynamic (vtables). Some systems allow retroactive implementation.
limits: Dynamic dispatch costs an indirect call
failures: Accidental dynamic dispatch in hot loops
whenNot: A single concrete type with no variation
myths: "Interfaces and typeclasses are identical" — coherence and retroactive impl differ
